backend_metrics.name: P132/P133 8-frame DROID-SLAM smoke
backend_metrics.scope: 8-frame bounded TorWIC Aisle_CW_Run_1 window, DROID-SLAM frontend/trajectory filler, global BA disabled because the smoke window is too short for proximity edges.
backend_metrics.claim_boundary: This proves the raw-vs-masked backend and evo evaluation path is executable. It does not prove masked input improves full-trajectory SLAM, map quality, or navigation.
backend_metrics.interpretation: On this tiny smoke window, raw and masked trajectories are effectively tied. The result should be reported as pipeline closure, not as a performance improvement claim.

backend_metrics.name: P134 64-frame DROID-SLAM global BA
backend_metrics.scope: 64-frame bounded TorWIC Aisle_CW_Run_1 window, DROID-SLAM with global BA enabled, Sim(3) alignment and scale correction in evo.
backend_metrics.claim_boundary: This proves a larger bounded DROID-SLAM raw-vs-masked backend run with global BA is executable. It does not prove masked input improves full-trajectory SLAM, map quality, or navigation.
backend_metrics.interpretation: Raw and masked trajectories are effectively tied on this 64-frame bounded global-BA run. The single-frame dynamic mask has negligible effect at this window scale.

backend_metrics.name: P135 existing semantic masks
backend_metrics.scope: 64-frame TorWIC Aisle_CW_Run_1, DROID-SLAM global BA, masked input built from existing forklift semantic frontend masks.
backend_metrics.claim_boundary: Metrics report bounded backend behavior only; they do not by themselves prove navigation benefit or full-map quality improvement.
backend_metrics.interpretation: Existing semantic masks are connected to the backend, but their temporal coverage is still sparse. The next step is dynamic-mask generation or tracking across more of the trajectory.

backend_metrics.name: P136 temporal mask stress test
backend_metrics.scope: 64-frame TorWIC Aisle_CW_Run_1, DROID-SLAM global BA, masked input built by nearest-frame temporal propagation of existing semantic forklift masks within +/-8 frames plus 4 px dilation.
backend_metrics.claim_boundary: Metrics report bounded backend behavior only; they do not by themselves prove navigation benefit or full-map quality improvement.
backend_metrics.interpretation: This is a diagnostic stress test, not a true detector output. It tests whether increasing temporal mask coverage makes the backend sensitive to dynamic masking.

backend_metrics.name: P137 optical-flow mask stress test
backend_metrics.scope: 64-frame TorWIC Aisle_CW_Run_1, DROID-SLAM global BA, masked input built by dense optical-flow propagation of existing semantic forklift masks within +/-8 frames plus 4 px dilation.
backend_metrics.claim_boundary: Metrics report bounded backend behavior only; they do not by themselves prove navigation benefit or full-map quality improvement.
backend_metrics.interpretation: This is a diagnostic stress test, not a true detector output. It tests whether optical-flow mask propagation improves over nearest-frame copying for backend sensitivity.

backend_metrics.name: P138 first-eight real semantic masks
backend_metrics.scope: 64-frame TorWIC Aisle_CW_Run_1, DROID-SLAM global BA, masked input built from existing per-frame frontend forklift masks on frames 000000-000007.
backend_metrics.claim_boundary: Metrics report bounded backend behavior only; they do not by themselves prove navigation benefit or full-map quality improvement.
backend_metrics.interpretation: This uses existing real frontend masks across the first eight frames rather than propagation. It tests whether denser true semantic masks change the backend trajectory metrics.

backend_metrics.name: P139 first-sixteen real semantic masks
backend_metrics.scope: 64-frame TorWIC Aisle_CW_Run_1, DROID-SLAM global BA, masked input built from real per-frame frontend forklift masks on frames 000000-000015.
backend_metrics.claim_boundary: Metrics report bounded backend behavior only; they do not by themselves prove navigation benefit or full-map quality improvement.
backend_metrics.interpretation: This uses real frontend masks across the first sixteen frames. It tests whether extending true semantic mask coverage beyond P138 changes backend trajectory metrics.

backend_metrics.name: P140 first-thirty-two real semantic masks
backend_metrics.scope: 64-frame TorWIC Aisle_CW_Run_1, DROID-SLAM global BA, masked input built from real per-frame frontend forklift masks on frames 000000-000031.
backend_metrics.claim_boundary: Metrics report bounded backend behavior only; they do not by themselves prove navigation benefit or full-map quality improvement.
backend_metrics.interpretation: This extends true frontend mask generation to half of the backend window. It tests whether substantially denser real semantic masks change the backend trajectory metrics.

backend_metrics.name: P141 dynamic SLAM window-selection diagnostic
backend_metrics.scope: Coverage-power analysis across P135-P140. Why 32/64 real masks remain trajectory-neutral (1280x720, 0.57% window feature budget) and what the next strongest experiment should be.
backend_metrics.claim_boundary: Diagnostic analysis only. No new backend run. Root cause: forklift occupies 0.63-1.39% per frame at 1280x720 (0.57% of DROID-SLAM feature budget across 64 frames). DROID-SLAM internal flow consistency already handles sparse dynamic features.
backend_metrics.interpretation: Even 32/64 real forklift masks produce trajectory-neutral results because the forklift covers only 0.63-1.39% of image area per frame (0.57% window-level). DROID-SLAM's internal RAFT flow consistency already filters sparse dynamic features. Next step: P142 strong dynamic segment screening (top-N highest-coverage frames only) rather than uniform full-window masking.

backend_metrics.name: P142 top4 concentrated dynamic masking
backend_metrics.scope: 64-frame TorWIC Aisle_CW_Run_1, DROID-SLAM global BA, real forklift masks only on 4 highest-coverage frames.
backend_metrics.claim_boundary: Concentrated masking of top-4 highest-coverage frames. Trajectory-neutral.
backend_metrics.interpretation: Top-4 concentrated masking produces identical ATE/RPE to raw. No trajectory impact.

backend_metrics.name: P142 top8 concentrated dynamic masking
backend_metrics.scope: 64-frame TorWIC Aisle_CW_Run_1, DROID-SLAM global BA, real forklift masks only on 8 highest-coverage frames.
backend_metrics.claim_boundary: Concentrated masking of top-8 highest-coverage frames. Slightly negative ΔAPE (−0.003mm), trajectory-neutral.
backend_metrics.interpretation: Top-8 concentrated masking produces a tiny negative ΔAPE (masked marginally better). Difference is below measurement noise.

backend_metrics.name: P142 top16 concentrated dynamic masking
backend_metrics.scope: 64-frame TorWIC Aisle_CW_Run_1, DROID-SLAM global BA, real forklift masks only on 16 highest-coverage frames.
backend_metrics.claim_boundary: Concentrated masking of top-16 highest-coverage frames. ΔAPE=−0.013mm — trajectory-neutral but sign pattern informative.
backend_metrics.interpretation: Concentrated top-16 masking shows a minor beneficial effect (−0.013mm) vs uniform 32-masking's harmful effect (+0.054mm). Sign asymmetry suggests boundary-feature loss in uniform masking is the dominant artifact.
